content.problemStatement:
You have XXX 010101 strings and YYY 101010 strings. You will form a new string SSS by first rearranging these strings in some order, and then concatenate them in this **rearranged order**.

Let f(S)f(S)f(S) denote the number of indices iii such that 1‚Č§i<‚ą£S‚ą£1 \le i < |S|1‚Č§i<‚ą£S‚ą£ and Si‚Č†Si+1S_i \ne S_{i + 1}Si‚ÄčÓÄ†=Si+1‚Äč. Find the **minimum possible** value of f(S)f(S)f(S).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input contains 222 integers - XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible value of f(S)f(S)f(S).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We rearrange as 101010, 010101, 101010 and concatenate to get S=100110S = 100110S=100110 which has f(S)=3f(S) = 3f(S)=3 as the indices i=1,3,5i = 1, 3, 5i=1,3,5 satisfy Si‚Č†Si+1S_i \ne S_{i + 1}Si‚ÄčÓÄ†=Si+1‚Äč.
Test case 222: S=01+10=0110S = 01 + 10 = 0110S=01+10=0110 has f(S)=2f(S) = 2f(S)=2.
